items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: Novo’s Wegovy pill reaches 1M patients, shattering expectations with $355M in sales
items.snippet: Analysts and investors alike had been eagerly awaiting sales figures for Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill. The answer blew past expectations by 86%.

items.title: CellCentric secures $220M in Europe’s biggest private biotech fundraise of 2026
items.snippet: Pfizer-backed cancer company CellCentric will use the cash to support the launch of a pivotal myeloma trial testing its potentially first-in-class oral treatment this year.

items.title: Bayer ends multi-year M&A drought with up to $2.45B Perfuse buy
items.snippet: For $300 million upfront, Bayer is purchasing Perfuse Therapeutics to advance an eye implant for glaucoma and diabetic retinopathy, marking the company’s first pharma acquisition since 2021.

items.title: Gilead guts Arcellx team as BioNTech plant closures will wipe out 1,860 jobs
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Viridian turns green again as new Phase 3 data vindicate thyroid eye disease drug
items.snippet: Viridian Therapeutics’ elegrobart normalized the degree of eye protrusion and improved double vision in a Phase 3 study. The company plans to file for approval in the first quarter of 2027.

items.title: J&J pushes dual-antibody IBD therapy into Phase 3 despite mid-stage fails
items.snippet: Johnson & Johnson plans to advance the co-antibody therapy, which combines its IL-23 blocker Tremfya and the TNF-alpha inhibitor Simponi, into late-stage testing for Crohn’s disease and ulcerative colitis.

items.title: Makary defends Replimune melanoma rejection again as FDA tensions build
items.snippet: Facing increasing pressure from both industry and the White House, FDA Commissioner Marty Makary said the strong bad press against him is “corporate spin” and that the agency has “followed the science.”

items.title: Pfizer, Lilly, more report Q1, FDA names acting CBER director and an ALS awakening
items.snippet: First quarter earnings continue to arrive, with analysts demanding more from cautious Pfizer and Eli Lilly expecting more revenue; the FDA taps Katherine Szarama as Vinay Prasad’s controversial FDA tenure ends; oncology veterans miss Richard Pazdur at the agency’s first adcomm in nine months; and QurAlis and Corcept T...

items.title: Founders face pressure from all sides as biotech crawls toward recovery
items.snippet: In Salt Lake City, biotech founders new and seasoned reflect on ways to ride out the industry’s challenges, such as sending cold emails to investors and learning to address leadership weaknesses.

items.title: Pfizer is a 2029 story but Q126 lays foundation for growth
items.snippet: A legal settlement has put wind behind Pfizer’s sales into 2029—at which point key obesity moves will take the helm.

items.title: BioNTech moves to close multiple manufacturing plants, affecting 1,860 jobs
items.snippet: The action affects BioNTech sites in Germany and Singapore, where the company expects to have excess capacity.

items.title: QurAlis’ Phase 2 data bolster case for genetic approach to sporadic ALS
items.snippet: Biogen’s Qalsody won FDA approval in 2023 to treat a rare, genetic form of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. On Tuesday, QurAlis presented interim Phase 2 data showing the potential of a similar drug to more broadly treat the neurodegenerative disease.

items.title: Pfizer stays the course for 2026 guidance, dulling impact of Q1 earnings beat
items.snippet: Despite the continued decline of Pfizer's COVID-19 products, shares stayed stable on Tuesday morning after the New York pharma reported a first quarter beat, which was led by Eliquis and Ibrance.

items.title: Madrigal picks up another siRNA asset for MASH, betting up to $1B with Arrowhead
items.snippet: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals has licensed from Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals an RNA interference candidate that targets a genetic mutation present in around 30% of patients with MASH.

items.title: Vertex’s quiet Q1 is calm before potentially ‘iconic’ renal evolution
items.snippet: While some analysts may regard Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ first quarter results as “unremarkable,” BMO Capital Markets wrote on Monday, the second half of 2026 could be big for the biotech, with the potential approval of IgAN therapy povetacicept.

items.title: Foundayo’s liver failure blip weighs down Lilly shares but analysts unconcerned
items.snippet: The selloff in Eli Lilly's shares was “overdone,” according to RBC Capital Markets, which noted that the overall safety profile of Foundayo remains favorable.

items.title: FDA uncertainty persists as search for Prasad successor at CBER drags on
items.snippet: The FDA is reportedly down to a handful of final candidates to lead CBER, with a potential selection expected in the coming month or two. For now, the appointment of acting director Katherine Szarama has not allayed the industry’s concerns.

items.title: IPF draws investor heat as biotechs target fibrosis halt, better tolerability
items.snippet: Novel targets aim to stop lung scarring—where current drugs only slow it—while improving tolerability and unlocking fibrosis in other organs next

items.title: Odyssey eyes $236M for renewed Nasdaq bid, Seaport docks on Nasdaq
items.snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

items.title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
items.snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...
